title: [Backend] Migration 018: Seed Week 3 Indian recipes (7 recipes)
description:
## HIGH — Seed 7 Week 3 recipes into the database

### Source
`/Users/pratham/Downloads/Indian Cooking Plan_ Weeks 3–5.pdf` — Pages 1-3 contain Week 3 recipes (Days 15-21).

### CRITICAL: Column names
**USE `skill_id` and `total_time` — NOT `skill` and `time`.**
Production broke last time because migration 017 used wrong column names. See commit `fb1ea04`.

### What to build

Add migration 018 in `database.ts` `initialize()` method, AFTER migration 017.

**SQL column order:**
```sql
INSERT INTO recipes (id, title, description, skill_id, difficulty, total_time, image_url, emoji, ingredients, instructions, tips, xp_reward) VALUES ...
```

### Image URLs (verified via Unsplash API — use these directly)
| Recipe ID | image_url |
|-----------|-----------|
| ic-poha | `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1644289450169-bc58aa16bacb?w=800&fit=crop&q=80` |
| ic-paneer-bhurji | `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1631452180539-96aca7d48617?w=800&fit=crop&q=80` |
| ic-matar-paneer | `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1708793873401-e8c6c153b76a?w=800&fit=crop&q=80` |
| ic-veg-pulao | `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1645177628172-a94c1f96e6db?w=800&fit=crop&q=80` |
| ic-bhindi-masala | `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1586981114766-708f09a71e20?w=800&fit=crop&q=80` |
| ic-masoor-dal | `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1668236534990-73c4ed23043c?w=800&fit=crop&q=80` |
| ic-week3-thali | `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1742281257707-0c7f7e5ca9c6?w=800&fit=crop&q=80` |

### 7 Recipes to seed:

**1. ic-poha — Poha (Flattened Rice Breakfast)**
- skill_id: `indian-cuisine`, difficulty: `beginner`, total_time: `20 minutes`, emoji: `🍛`, xp_reward: 100
- Unsplash search: `poha Indian breakfast flattened rice`
- description: `Quick and popular Indian breakfast made from flattened rice with onions, spices, and peanuts`
- ingredients: `["2 cups thick poha (flattened rice)", "1 medium onion (finely chopped)", "1 small potato (diced small)", "1 green chili (slit)", "10-12 peanuts", "1/2 tsp mustard seeds", "8-10 curry leaves", "1/4 tsp turmeric", "Salt to taste", "2 tbsp oil", "Juice of half a lemon", "Fresh cilantro for garnish"]`
- instructions: `["Rinse poha in water briefly and drain — do not soak", "Chop onion, green chili, and dice potatoes small", "Heat oil and add mustard seeds — wait until they splutter", "Add curry leaves and peanuts, fry 30 seconds", "Add chopped onion and diced potato, saute until potato softens (5 min)", "Add turmeric and salt, stir well", "Add drained poha and mix gently until heated through (3-4 min)", "Squeeze lemon juice and garnish with cilantro", "Serve hot"]`
- tips: `["Rinse poha briefly — don''t soak or it becomes mushy", "Medium-thick poha holds its shape better than thin variety", "Lemon juice at the end is essential for authentic taste", "Add peanuts early so they get crispy"]`

**2. ic-paneer-bhurji — Paneer Bhurji (Paneer Scramble)**
- skill_id: `indian-cuisine`, difficulty: `beginner`, total_time: `20 minutes`, emoji: `🧀`, xp_reward: 100
- Unsplash search: `paneer Indian cottage cheese scramble`
- description: `Scrambled paneer with spices — like a vegetarian egg bhurji with Indian cottage cheese`
- ingredients: `["200g fresh paneer (crumbled or grated)", "1 onion (finely chopped)", "2 tomatoes (finely chopped)", "1 green chili (chopped)", "1 tsp cumin seeds", "1 tsp ginger-garlic paste", "1/4 tsp turmeric", "1/2 tsp garam masala", "1/4 cup green peas (optional)", "2 tbsp oil", "Salt to taste", "Fresh coriander for garnish"]`
- instructions: `["Grate or crumble fresh paneer into small pieces", "Finely chop onion, tomatoes, and green chili", "Heat oil and add cumin seeds until they splutter", "Add chopped onions and saute until soft (3-4 min)", "Add ginger-garlic paste and turmeric, cook 1 minute", "Add chopped tomatoes, cook until soft and masala thickens (3-4 min)", "Stir in crumbled paneer and peas", "Cook 3-4 minutes, seasoning with salt and garam masala", "Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot"]`
- tips: `["Fresh paneer works best — avoid store-bought hard paneer", "Don''t overcook paneer or it becomes rubbery", "The tomato masala should be thick before adding paneer", "Serve with roti or as a sandwich filling"]`

**3. ic-matar-paneer — Matar Paneer (Peas with Paneer Curry)**
- skill_id: `indian-cuisine`, difficulty: `intermediate`, total_time: `35 minutes`, emoji: `🍛`, xp_reward: 100
- Unsplash search: `matar paneer peas curry Indian`
- description: `North Indian curry of peas and paneer cubes in a spiced onion-tomato-cashew gravy`
- ingredients: `["200g paneer (cubed)", "1 cup green peas", "2 onions (roughly chopped)", "2 tomatoes (roughly chopped)", "3 garlic cloves", "8-10 cashews", "1 tsp cumin seeds", "1 bay leaf", "1/2 tsp turmeric", "1 tsp red chili powder", "1 tsp garam masala", "1 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek)", "1/2 cup water or cream", "2 tbsp oil", "Salt to taste", "Fresh coriander"]`
- instructions: `["Blend sauteed onions, tomatoes, garlic, and cashews into a smooth paste", "In a pan, heat oil and saute cumin seeds and bay leaf", "Add the blended paste and cook 5 minutes until oil separates", "Add turmeric, chili powder, and salt", "Add water or cream for desired gravy consistency", "Stir in green peas and cubed paneer", "Simmer 5-7 minutes until peas are cooked", "Finish with kasuri methi and garam masala", "Garnish with fresh coriander"]`
- tips: `["Cashews make the gravy creamy and rich", "Blend the base very smooth for restaurant-style texture", "Kasuri methi (fenugreek leaves) is the signature flavor", "Paneer should be added last so it stays soft"]`

**4. ic-veg-pulao — Vegetable Pulao (One-pot Rice)**
- skill_id: `indian-cuisine`, difficulty: `beginner`, total_time: `30 minutes`, emoji: `🍚`, xp_reward: 100
- Unsplash search: `vegetable pulao pilaf Indian rice`
- description: `Aromatic one-pot basmati rice dish with mixed vegetables and whole spices`
- ingredients: `["1 cup basmati rice", "1 carrot (diced)", "1/4 cup green beans (chopped)", "1/4 cup green peas", "1 onion (sliced)", "1 tsp cumin seeds", "1 bay leaf", "2 cardamom pods", "1 small cinnamon stick", "1 tsp ginger-garlic paste", "2 cups water", "1 tbsp oil or ghee", "Salt to taste"]`
- instructions: `["Rinse and soak basmati rice for 20 minutes, then drain", "Chop carrots, beans, and onions", "Heat oil/ghee in a pot, add cumin, bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom", "Saute sliced onions until translucent (3-4 min)", "Add ginger-garlic paste and chopped veggies, cook 1-2 minutes", "Drain rice and add to pot, stir to coat with spices", "Add 2 cups water and salt, bring to a boil", "Cover and cook on low flame until water is absorbed (15 min)", "Fluff with fork and serve"]`
- tips: `["Soaking rice ensures long, separate grains", "Don''t stir rice while cooking", "Whole spices infuse the rice — remove before eating", "Use ghee for richer flavor"]`

**5. ic-bhindi-masala — Bhindi Masala (Okra Stir-fry)**
- skill_id: `indian-cuisine`, difficulty: `beginner`, total_time: `25 minutes`, emoji: `🫑`, xp_reward: 100
- Unsplash search: `okra bhindi masala Indian vegetable`
- description: `Dry stir-fried okra with onions, tomatoes, and spices — crispy and flavorful`
- ingredients: `["250g okra (bhindi)", "1 onion (finely chopped)", "1 tomato (diced)", "1 tsp cumin seeds", "1/4 tsp turmeric", "1/2 tsp red chili powder", "1/2 tsp garam masala", "Salt to taste", "2 tbsp oil", "Lemon juice", "Fresh coriander"]`
- instructions: `["Trim and slice okra into 1-inch pieces", "Heat oil and add cumin seeds until they splutter", "Add finely chopped onion and saute until soft (3-4 min)", "Add okra, turmeric, and salt", "Fry on medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until tender and lightly browned (10-12 min)", "Add diced tomato, chili powder, and garam masala", "Cook 2-3 more minutes until tomato softens", "Finish with lemon juice and fresh coriander"]`
- tips: `["Wash and completely dry okra before cutting to reduce sliminess", "Don''t cover the pan — steam makes okra slimy", "Cook on medium-high heat for crispy results", "Add tomato at the end so okra stays crisp"]`

**6. ic-masoor-dal — Masoor Dal Tadka (Red Lentil)**
- skill_id: `indian-cuisine`, difficulty: `beginner`, total_time: `25 minutes`, emoji: `🍲`, xp_reward: 100
- Unsplash search: `masoor dal red lentil Indian`
- description: `Quick-cooking red lentils finished with a fragrant cumin-garlic oil tempering`
- ingredients: `["1/2 cup masoor dal (red lentils)", "2 cups water", "1/4 tsp turmeric", "Salt to taste", "1 tbsp ghee or oil", "1 tsp cumin seeds", "3 garlic cloves (chopped)", "1 dried red chili", "Fresh coriander for garnish"]`
- instructions: `["Rinse masoor dal and boil with turmeric and salt until soft (10-12 min)", "Mash lightly with a ladle — dal should be slightly soupy", "TADKA: Heat ghee or oil in a small pan", "Add cumin seeds and wait until they splutter", "Add chopped garlic and dried red chili, fry until golden", "Pour the sizzling tadka over the cooked dal", "Simmer everything together for 2 more minutes", "Adjust seasoning and garnish with coriander"]`
- tips: `["Masoor dal cooks fastest of all lentils — no soaking needed", "The tadka should sizzle when poured over the dal", "Use ghee for more authentic flavor", "Dal thickens as it cools — keep it slightly runny"]`

**7. ic-week3-thali — Week 3 Thali (Combined Meal)**
- skill_id: `indian-cuisine`, difficulty: `beginner`, total_time: `60 minutes`, emoji: `🍽️`, xp_reward: 150
- Unsplash search: `Indian thali meal plate`
- description: `Combine this week''s recipes into a balanced Indian thali — rotis, rice, dal, and sabzi`
- ingredients: `["Ingredients for roti (from Day 8)", "Ingredients for plain rice", "Ingredients for masoor dal", "Leftover paneer bhurji or matar paneer", "Leftover bhindi masala", "Fresh salad (cucumber, tomato, onion)", "Lemon wedges"]`
- instructions: `["PLAN YOUR TIMING: Start dal first (longest cook time)", "While dal simmers, start rice", "Reheat paneer bhurji or matar paneer from this week", "Reheat bhindi masala", "Make fresh rotis last — they are best served hot", "ASSEMBLY: Place 2-3 rotis on a large plate", "Add a bowl of rice in the center", "Place dal in a small bowl", "Add paneer dish and bhindi on the sides", "Garnish with fresh salad, lemon wedges", "Enjoy your Week 3 thali!"]`
- tips: `["This week you learned paneer, pulao, and new vegetables", "Reheating sabzis is part of real Indian home cooking", "A thali is about variety and balance", "Note how each dish uses skills from this week"]`

### Migration code template
```ts
if (!(await this.isMigrationApplied('018_indian_plan_week3'))) {
  await this.pool.query(`
    INSERT INTO recipes (id, title, description, skill_id, difficulty, total_time, image_url, emoji, ingredients, instructions, tips, xp_reward) VALUES
    -- ... all 7 recipes ...
    ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
  `);
  await this.recordMigration('018_indian_plan_week3');
  logger.info('Indian cooking plan Week 3 recipes seeded');
}
```

### Subtasks
1. Search Unsplash for each of the 7 dishes and collect image URLs (verify each loads)
2. Write migration 018 block in database.ts after migration 017
3. Use correct columns: `skill_id`, `total_time` (NOT `skill`, `time`) — **THIS BROKE PROD LAST TIME**
4. Escape single quotes with `''` in SQL strings
5. Escape `%%` for percent signs in template literals
6. Verify TypeScript compiles: `npx tsc --noEmit`
7. Test locally: `docker compose down -v && docker compose up`

### Acceptance criteria

- Given `docker compose down -v && docker compose up`, all migrations apply cleanly

title: [Backend] DB Migration 021: multi-photo schema
description:
## HIGH — Alter user_recipe_photos to support up to 3 photos per recipe

### What's wrong
`user_recipe_photos` has `UNIQUE(user_id, recipe_id)` constraint — only 1 photo per recipe per user. The `upsertRecipePhoto` method replaces the existing photo. We need to support up to 3.

### What to build

**1. Add migration 021 in `database.ts` `initializeDatabase()`:**
```sql
-- Migration 021: Multi-photo support
ALTER TABLE user_recipe_photos ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS photo_number SMALLINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1;
ALTER TABLE user_recipe_photos DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS user_recipe_photos_user_id_recipe_id_key;
ALTER TABLE user_recipe_photos ADD CONSTRAINT user_recipe_photos_user_id_recipe_id_photo_number_key UNIQUE(user_id, recipe_id, photo_number);
ALTER TABLE user_recipe_photos ADD CONSTRAINT user_recipe_photos_photo_number_check CHECK (photo_number BETWEEN 1 AND 3);
```

**2. Update DatabaseService methods:**

- `getUserRecipePhotos(userId)` — add `photo_number` to SELECT, ORDER BY `recipe_id, photo_number`
- New `addRecipePhoto(userId, recipeId, photoUrl, storageKey)` method:
  1. `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_recipe_photos WHERE user_id = $1 AND recipe_id = $2` — if >= 3, throw error
  2. `SELECT COALESCE(MAX(photo_number), 0) + 1 FROM user_recipe_photos WHERE user_id = $1 AND recipe_id = $2` — get next number
  3. INSERT with that photo_number, return the new record
- New `deleteRecipePhotoByNumber(userId, recipeId, photoNumber)` — delete specific photo, return storage_key for cleanup
- Keep existing `upsertRecipePhoto` but have it call `addRecipePhoto` internally (backwards compat)
- Keep existing `deleteRecipePhoto` working (deletes all photos for that recipe)

**3. Update `getUserRecipePhotos` return shape:**
Return: `Array<{ recipe_id: string, photo_url: string, storage_key: string, photo_number: number, uploaded_at: string }>`

### Subtasks
1. Add migration 021 SQL to initializeDatabase migration list
2. Update getUserRecipePhotos to include photo_number, order by it
3. Create addRecipePhoto method with count check and auto photo_number
4. Create deleteRecipePhotoByNumber method
5. Update upsertRecipePhoto to delegate to addRecipePhoto

### Depends on
none

### Acceptance criteria
- Given migration 021 runs, When checking user_recipe_photos, Then photo_number column exists with CHECK(1-3)
- Given a user with 0 photos for a recipe, When addRecipePhoto is called, Then photo_number = 1
- Given a user with 2 photos for a recipe, When addRecipePhoto is called, Then photo_number = 3
- Given a user with 3 photos for a recipe, When addRecipePhoto is called, Then error is thrown
- Given deleteRecipePhotoByNumber(user, recipe, 2), When executed, Then only photo_number=2 is deleted

### Files to create/modify
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/database.ts` (modify)

### What's wrong
Currently cookbook mode shows 1 PhotoNode per recipe. Users should see a node for each uploaded photo, plus an empty upload node if under 3.

### What to build

**1. Update `hooks/use-recipes.ts` — `useUserPhotos()`:**

title: [Backend] Multi-photo API routes + feed photos
description:
## HIGH — Update photo upload routes and feed endpoints for multi-photo support

### What's wrong
Photo upload route uses `upsertRecipePhoto` (replaces photo). DELETE route removes the only photo. Feed returns single `photo_url` per post. All need multi-photo support.

### What to build

**1. Update `routes/photos.ts`:**
- POST `/recipes/:id/photos`:
  - Use `addRecipePhoto()` instead of `upsertRecipePhoto()`
  - If count >= 3, return `{ success: false, error: 'Maximum 3 photos per recipe' }` with status 400
  - Return `{ success: true, data: { photoUrl, photoNumber } }`
  - On first photo, create feed post (existing). On 2nd/3rd, update existing post's photo_url to latest but don't create new post
- DELETE `/recipes/:id/photos/:photoNumber`:
  - New route parameter `photoNumber` (validate 1-3)
  - Use `deleteRecipePhotoByNumber(userId, recipeId, photoNumber)`
  - Clean up storage file
  - Return `{ success: true }`
- GET `/users/me/photos`:
  - Return grouped by recipe: `{ [recipeId]: Array<{ photoUrl, photoNumber, uploadedAt }> }`

**2. Update feed endpoints in `routes/social.ts`:**
- For `photo_upload` type posts, LEFT JOIN with `user_recipe_photos`:
  ```sql
  SELECT up.*, 
    COALESCE(
      (SELECT json_agg(urp.photo_url ORDER BY urp.photo_number)
       FROM user_recipe_photos urp 
       WHERE urp.user_id = up.user_id AND urp.recipe_id = up.recipe_id),
      '[]'::json
    ) as photos
  FROM user_posts up ...
  ```
- Map `photos` into the response for each post
- For non-photo posts, `photos` defaults to `[]`

**3. Update post creation logic:**
- `hasPhotoUploadPost(userId, recipeId)` — already exists, use it
- On photo 2/3: don't create new post, just update existing post's `photo_url` to the latest
- SQL: `UPDATE user_posts SET photo_url = $1 WHERE user_id = $2 AND recipe_id = $3 AND post_type = 'photo_upload'`

### Subtasks
1. Update POST /recipes/:id/photos to use addRecipePhoto with count check
2. Add DELETE /recipes/:id/photos/:photoNumber route
3. Update GET /users/me/photos to return grouped arrays
4. Update feed query to LEFT JOIN photos array for photo_upload posts
5. Update post creation: update existing post on 2nd/3rd photo

### Depends on
task_163 (for migration and new DB methods)

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a recipe with 2 photos, When uploading a 3rd, Then response includes photoNumber: 3
- Given a recipe with 3 photos, When uploading another, Then 400 error returned
- Given DELETE /recipes/pancakes/photos/2, When executed, Then only photo 2 is removed
- Given feed endpoint returns a photo_upload post, When post has 3 photos, Then `photos` array has 3 URLs
- Given a non-photo post on feed, When returned, Then `photos` is empty array

### Files to create/modify
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/photos.ts` (modify)
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/social.ts` (modify)
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/database.ts` (modify — minor updates)

```sql
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS has_completed_onboarding BOOLEAN DEFAULT false;
```
`preferences` JSONB column already exists and stores `skill_level`. `AuthUser.profile.skill_level` already typed as `'beginner' | 'intermediate' | 'advanced'`.

**3. Create `routes/demo.ts` (NO auth):**

- `GET /api/v1/demo/people` — 6 sample profiles with varied stats/levels

**4. Register routes in `routes/index.ts`**
